fieldValues.value: Strengthen `ext:repository` archive spec to require deterministic ZIP production. A conforming archive producer MUST: (1) sort entries in byte-lexicographic order of forward-slash-normalised paths; (2) set `last mod file time` and `last mod file date` to `0x0000` in all local file headers and central directory entries; (3) use only Deflate (method 8) or Store (method 0), with consistent per-file choice across invocations; (4) write empty `extra` fields (no host metadata); (5) set the Language encoding flag (bit 11) to 1. Given identical repository content, a conformant producer MUST produce a byte-for-byte identical `.srs` file across invocations of the same implementation. Cross-implementation byte identity is not guaranteed because the Store/Deflate choice is implementation-defined.

Deterministic archives enable content-addressing, deduplication, and reproducible builds. Standard ZIP with deterministic entry ordering and zeroed metadata achieves this while remaining compatible with all existing ZIP tooling, unlike content-addressed tarballs or other formats. Alphabetic sorting without exceptions (including manifest.json) allows binary search over the central directory without special-casing.
